title: Waga Energy develops a Renewable Natural Gas project in Florida
content: Hillsborough County has selected Waga Energy to build, own, operate, and maintain a renewable natural gas production unit at the Southeast County Landfill in Lithia, Florida. The plant will use Waga Energyâ€™s patented WAGABOX technology to upgrade landfill gas into pipeline-quality RNG. Once operational, it will process up to 3,000 standard cubic feet per minute of landfill gas and produce over 610,000 MMBtu, or 180 GWh, of RNG per year, delivering gas to the local pipeline for at least 20 years. The project is expected to reduce up to 47,500 tons of COâ‚‚ equivalent emissions annually due to the displacement of fossil fuels, equivalent to avoiding the COâ‚‚ emissions from 4.2 million gallons of diesel consumed. By producing pipeline-quality RNG, the project supports the circular economy and reduces reliance on fossil fuels with a local, reliable, and renewable source of energy provided to the community. The Southeast County Landfill is owned by Hillsborough County and operated by WM, and it accepts more than 400,000 tons of waste annually. WAGABOX combines membrane filtration with cryogenic distillation to maximize renewable energy production regardless of landfill gas variations in flow rate and composition. â€œWe are proud to be partnering with Hillsborough County on this major project, and deploying our first WAGABOX unit in Florida near our U.S. commercial headquarters,â€ said GuÃ©naÃ«l Prince, co-founder and CEO of Waga Energy Inc., highlighting energy resilience and decarbonization for Floridaâ€™s communities
author: Analysts Account
